title: rubus hirtus var. foliosus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. foliosus, also known as the hairy bramble or leafy bramble, is a variety of the Rubus hirtus plant, which is a type of wild raspberry native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and stems of Rubus hirtus var. foliosus have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections, providing health benefits like reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
